EFAtools 0.7.1.9000
  • Get started
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Replicate SPSS and R psych results with EFAtools
  • Changelog

Print COMPARE object

Source: R/print.COMPARE.R
print.COMPARE.Rd

Print Method showing a summarized output of the COMPARE function.

# S3 method for class 'COMPARE'
print(x, ...)

Arguments

x

list. An object of class COMPARE to be printed

...

Further arguments for print.

Examples

# A type SPSS EFA to mimick the SPSS implementation
EFA_SPSS_5 <- EFA(IDS2_R, n_factors = 5, type = "SPSS")
#> Warning: Reached maximum number of iterations without convergence. Results may not be interpretable.

# A type psych EFA to mimick the psych::fa() implementation
EFA_psych_5 <- EFA(IDS2_R, n_factors = 5, type = "psych")

# compare the two
COMPARE(EFA_SPSS_5$unrot_loadings, EFA_psych_5$unrot_loadings,
        x_labels = c("SPSS", "psych"))
#> Mean [min, max] absolute difference:  0.0017 [ 0.0000,  0.0090]
#> Median absolute difference:  0.0009
#> Max decimals where all numbers are equal: 1
#> Minimum number of decimals provided: 17
#> 
#>    	  F1  	  F2  	  F3  	  F4  	  F5  
#> GS 	 0.0004	 0.0002	-0.0002	 0.0002	-0.0034
#> PL 	-0.0000	 0.0001	 0.0007	-0.0003	 0.0001
#> TC 	 0.0007	 0.0021	-0.0058	 0.0001	 0.0024
#> CB 	-0.0007	-0.0039	-0.0005	 0.0043	-0.0024
#> NL 	 0.0020	-0.0090	-0.0056	 0.0032	 0.0016
#> NLM	-0.0018	 0.0069	 0.0082	 0.0034	-0.0011
#> GF 	-0.0002	 0.0003	 0.0036	-0.0021	-0.0003
#> RGF	 0.0004	 0.0011	 0.0049	 0.0020	 0.0011
#> CM 	-0.0001	-0.0001	 0.0004	-0.0009	 0.0011
#> EP 	-0.0001	-0.0001	 0.0003	-0.0010	 0.0009
#> CA 	-0.0001	 0.0006	-0.0020	-0.0027	 0.0009
#> OP 	-0.0001	 0.0007	-0.0016	-0.0025	 0.0008
#> RS 	-0.0001	 0.0012	-0.0013	-0.0032	-0.0010
#> DP 	-0.0001	 0.0009	 0.0004	-0.0024	-0.0009
